title: pedicularis comosa subsp. campestris
intro: Pedicularis comosa subsp. campestris, also known as Mountain Lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant compounds. Mountain Lousewort is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its health benefits, such as relieving coughs, bronchitis, and sinus congestion.
